back: Tachycardia, orthostatic BP changes, dry mucosa, decreased urine output, concentrated urine—always interpret in clinical context.

front: SBAR handoff: what does S stand for?
back: Situation—a concise statement of what is going on right now.

front: Before a blood transfusion at the bedside
back: Two qualified identifiers, product verification, vital signs per policy, and line confirmation—follow institutional transfusion protocol.

front: mL/hr from total volume and minutes
back: Rate (mL/hr) = volume (mL) ÷ time in hours. Convert minutes to hours when needed.
Establish a patent airway, support breathing, and provide oxygen/ventilation as indicated before focusing on detailed history.
